stemHtml: Flying a daytime visual approach with reduced visibility due to fog, rain or haze can give the illusion that the aircraft is..

options.a: at a greater distance from the runway than it actually is, and the pilot tends to fly a shallow glide path and land long.
options.b: faster than it actually is on the approach, and the pilot risks reducing power too early and landing short of the runway.
options.c: closer to the runway than it actually is, and the pilot has a tendency to fly a much steeper approach.
options.d: lower than it actually is on the approach, and the pilot risks landing long and overshooting the runway by staying higher.
